tatao.message: Thank you Ta-Tao. You have been a great colleague. Grateful for your support over the years.

tatao.story: When it comes to scheduling grad classes, Ta-Tao can never teach on Wednesday night. It's badminton night at the community center. Badminton runs in his veins.
tatao.message:
As long as you keep playing badminton, I'll know where to find you. Wednesday or Sundays at the community center playing badminton. I'll miss seeing your smile as you walk around the building. And introducing me to snacks, sweets or fruit from other countries that we can buy from Costco. ;)
I really enjoyed your workshops. You made information systems less intimidating.
Thanks for your presence in the SBA! Your perspective, your willingness to share, and your concern for our students has made a difference for me. We will miss you!

tatao.story: Ta-Tao is a wonderful colleague and mentor to me. He often checks in to see how things are going and always has a great story to share. One that really sticks with me is how he bravely “protected” his wife from mice and successfully caught those naughty little troublemakers!:)
tatao.message: Congratulations on your retirement! You’ve been such a dedicated and passionate educator to the students. I hope you enjoy this next chapter and have plenty of time to do all the fun things you love!
Congratulations on your retirement! I hope to keep hearing from you about AI.

Dear Ta-Tao,
You have been a very special part of the gift my work in the business school has been and the joy it has brought me.  I am humbled with gratitude.  From the early days-beginning with the day you walked into my office to find me there, instead of Terry- your tender and genuine heart won me over.   I am honored to call you colleague and friend and I want you to know you are deeply valued and cherished, by me.  
Ta-Tao, as you do, continue to seek peace; find it and know it and remember it.  I believe that you give yourself that gift with your retirement.  
Go play now!
With appreciation and affection,
Maureen

tatao.story: I will always appreciate Ta-Tao's smile and words of wisdom, especially when it came to food and travel.
tatao.message: Ta-Tao, Wishing you all the best with the next chapter of your journey. You have always been helpful, kind and very witty. May you continue to be as awesome and encouraging as you are.

Ta-Tao, I've appreciated your advice as newer faculty. I remember when it first started getting cold during my first semester and you asked me how I was doing. I told you I was cold. You told me to take up skiing because it made the winters more fun. I appreciate your sound and fun advice. I'm going to miss you! 
-Stephanie
Ta-Tao, thanks for being an awesome and supportive colleague!

tatao.story: There is no better advocate for travel than Ta-Tao! For the last several years, he always has the most amazing photos and gifts he brings back from his travels!
tatao.message: Who is going to look after my health now?! I guess I will just stock tea, apples, and good vibes in the office. When I feel wiped, I will go on a walk and imagine that you told me to do it!

tatao.story: In 2009. Ta-Tao used his contacts and influence to help me get connected with Chang Gung University in Taiwan.  I was able to spend three amazing weeks there during my first sabbatical teaching some classes and connecting with faculty and students.  From this, I was able to collaborate with new friends on two papers.  I recall Ta-Tao working tirelessly and enthusiastically to make this experience in Taiwan for me possible.
tatao.message: Thank you Ta-Tao for your help and friendship over the years.  You are an amazing colleague and friend and I wish the best as you begin this new chapter!
Ta-Tao, I will miss your positive energy and zest for life! Best wishes for a happy retirement. :)

tatao.story: Ta-Tao loved badminton and pickleball, and I was always the spoiled youngster who had some other small plans for big nights.
tatao.message: Congratulations on your retirement, Ta-Tao! It's SKI TIME!

tatao.story: His love for BBQ chicken wings.
tatao.message: Thanks for being a wonderful colleague. I appreciate all of the wonderful your advice, working on bringing faculty up to speed on AI, and your passion to promote badminton and pickleball in the SBA.

tatao.story: Ta-Tao helps ease my transition to Spokane - the beef noodle soup from his wife is excellent!
tatao.message: Knowing you, I am sure that you will continue to do some professional works after retirement...:)

tatao.story:
My fondest memories are lunches in the lounge when Ta-Tao offered to pick up Chinese takeout. What a wonderful community builder! I loved sharing food and conversation with him and other Jepson characters like Hickman and Shrader.

I also loved running into Ta-Tao in a random airport when we were both returning from summer trips to Europe. The world is both big and small!

Also, Ta-Tao was the first colleague I told that I was pregnant with Marie. He had arranged for me to teach in Taiwan, and I had to tell him that I wouldn't be able to go after all. He was very sweet and supportive about it, of course.
tatao.message:
My dear friend, Ta-Tao,

I am SO happy that you have this chance to move on to your next great adventure! How wonderful for you! We will all miss your exuberant laugh, your thoughtful questions, and the extra effort you've put in to help the non-techies among us get more comfortable with technology. Future Zags will miss the chance to learn from such a knowledgeable professor. But all of us, and all your past students, are better off for having known and learned from you, and that's the best gift you could have given us! 

Thank you and best wishes in all the days ahead!!! I wish you joy, peace, and only good surprises!

Peggy Sue 

PS Please come back for lunch or coffee!

Ta-Tao - this place will not be the same without you.  We will miss seeing you in the hallways and telling us about all the new and nifty tech!
-Sara

tatao.story: Ta-tao, your dedication to learning new technologies has been truly inspiring. I will miss having such a wonderful colleague to share learning experiences with.
tatao.message: Wish you all the best in the next chapter of your life and have fun!

tatao.story: Badminton, Chinese lunch delivery from Hong Kong Express which he organized for a while and was so awesome, and learning Power BI.
tatao.message: Ta-Tao, you will be missed! Your positive attitude has made working at Gonzaga a great place to be! I appreciate your encouragement to play badminton, learn Power BI, and keep challenging our students and ourselves.  Thank you!
Cheers to retirement! May this next chapter be full of joy, freedom, and just enough plans to keep things interesting.

tatao.story: You discouraged me from moving to Spokane! You were so honest, even though I was a job candidate. I still remeber that! haha
tatao.message: Ta-Tao, whenever you are around, I always feel like there is someone who genuinely cares and someone I can share things with.

Thank you for all the support, mentorship, and guidance in the last two years. It is very appreciated and means so much more than you know. Your hard work and dedication have set a high standard for us all. Thank you for everything, and enjoy this new chapter of your life. May your retirement be everything you've dreamed of and more. We will miss you

Ta-Tao, I can't believe you won't be my neighbor next year! I'm so glad I got to know you better over the last several years, and even though I'm going to miss seeing you daily, I'm excited for you to have the time to do the things you're looking forward to - without deadlines! Best wishes!

tatao.story: Ta Tao’s dedication to continuous learning and his passion for incorporating new technologies into his teaching have left a lasting impact on both students and colleagues.
tatao.message: Congratulations on your well-deserved retirement! Wishing you all the best in your next chapter.

It’s been such a joy working with you, Ta-Tao! You always had time for a quick chat and were always willing to be to share your knowledge with me. Your passion and dedication to your students stood out to me. You will be missed!
Congratulations on your retirement, Ta-Tao! I hope you come by my office again soon to practice our Spanish! Felicidades 🥳 te vas a divertir mucho en tus viajes! ✈️ - Mariella

tatao.story: You gave me a start as an adjunct and when I inquired about becoming a full-time professor, you were very direct with me about the need to get my Ph.D.  It was the clearest feedback I received and you didn't flinch about the commitment. Without this direct feedback, I would have taken much longer to understand what I needed to do in order to achieve my goals.
tatao.message: As I have gotten to know you well over the years, you have a great balance of wise principles that you are dedicated to with an open generous heart for the important aspects of life.  I am really going to miss working with you and thank you for all you have done for me, the discipline, the SBA and university.  Please do not be a stranger!  John

jane.message: Wishing you a restful and happy retirement. Thank you for your immense and valuable help and support over two decades. Best wishes and god bless. Warm regards. Ashish

jane.story: I always knew I could count on Jane to have an answer for me. She helped me with the accelerated students.
jane.message: I loved attending the staff get togethers knowing you would be there. You put people at ease and that's a great trait to have.

Wow - where to start? Thank you, Jane. Your help in bringing all of us new advisors up to speed, supporting as we faced more complicated situations, and your dedication to helping our transfer students make the transition to Gonzaga made a real difference. We will miss you and wish you the best in the next steps of your journey!

jane.story: Whenever I have questions about advising, Jane has always been gracious and incredibly helpful!
jane.message: Congrats, Jane! Wishing you all the very best in this exciting next chapter!
Thank you for always being so encouraging and bringing your life stories into the conversation. I'll miss my office neighbor!
Congratulations! I don't know what we'll do without you!

Dear Jane,
You have been a very special part of the gift my work in the business school has been and the joy it has brought me.  I am humbled with gratitude.  You have been the gold standard for the school, setting the bar for excellence, pulling always for the school.  You’ve been an inspiration and role-model to me.
I will always be grateful.   I am honored to call you colleague and friend and I want you to know you are deeply valued and cherished, by me.  
Go play now!
With appreciation and affection,
Maureen

jane.story: What can I say or share about Jane. Jane is amazing and I am so proud of her.
jane.message: Jane, I want to say, "Thank you" for always being inviting and the life of the party in Jepson. Your knowledge of Gonzaga is crazy amazing and I will miss our chats on family, travel, and life. You have impacted me in more ways words could not express. I will miss you, but know you will enjoy your grandbabies, travel, a bit of slower mornings but still staying active and brighten others' day. Thank you...I appreciate you. I'm very proud of you.

Jane, 
I'm going to miss seeing you at all the SBA events. You're always one of my favorite people to talk too. Thank you for teaching me how to get from Hemmingson to Jepson by cutting through the buildings when it snows. You're truly the best! 
-Stephanie
Your energy, joy, and positive spirit is contagious! Thank you for being an incredible colleague!

jane.story: Jane's ability to laugh at the funny and absurd made her the first person I would go to talk to in an SBA social event.
jane.message: You're my favorite, and I am going to miss seeing HONEY in the parking lot and miss hearing your insight and perspectives.

jane.story: Early in my career (2002-2005...don't recall exactly when), I recall that we held an SBA social at Jane's home on the South Hill.  I have fond memories of that evening and the Hession's amazing home and warm hospitality.
jane.message: I know we already talked about this transition for you, but I wish you health and every happiness going forward.  You've certainly earned it and will be greatly missed.
Jane, You will be sorely missed! I love your joyful attitude toward life, and I appreciate your care for our students and willingness to help others. Best wishes for a wonderful retirement! :)

jane.story: I met Jane's husband at the 2024 Sasquatch Soiree, and I was so surprised that he looks just like Jane! Indeed, happy couples do turn out to resemble each other, and I routinely share this story with my newlywed wife when we have the same thing for lunch, even though we are 2,500 miles apart.
jane.message: Congratulations on your retirement, Jane! I will truly miss you.
Jane, thank you for being a wonderful friend and colleague. I hope you have a wonderful time in retirement and get to spend as much time with your family.

I have so enjoyed having you around this past year (+) that I have worked in the SBA.  You will be missed and I hope you stop in to see us once in a while.  Enjoy your retirement - you deserve the best!

jane.story:
I have so many! One favorite is a meeting we had during my freshman year when she called Fr. Siconolfi to ask why he had not invited me to join the Honor's Program. He said something to the effect that because I had not taken AP and Honors classes in high school, it didn't appear that I was prepared to be an Honors student. Jane pointed out that my tiny rural Montana high school did not offer AP and Honors classes, but that I had other credentials that suggested that I would be a good candidate. (I won't flex those now because that's lame, but she wasn't wrong!) In any case, she insisted that he grant me an interview to be considered for an unprecedented post-matriculation acceptance to the Honors program. I met with the guy and decided "NO THANK YOU!" but I was very grateful that she went to bat for me anyway. I cried when I found out I didn't get to keep her as my advisor (silly freshman!), but Boz turned out to be ok too! :)

I also have a fond memory of walking around Chicago together when were there for CJBE. We went into Bloomingdale's (a store I'd never set foot in!) where I bought a fancy spork, and she taught me about CMFM shoes! 

I also really fondly remember SBA parties and Thanksgivings and even a couple of game watches at the Hession house. Those were lovely times. So special!
jane.message:
Dearest Janie,

I am SO happy for you that you have decided to move on to your next great adventure! How wonderful! We will all miss your very contagious laugh, your thoughtfulness, your style (future runway model!) and your constancy in keeping us focused on the mission and on student success. You are the embodiment of what cura personalis looks like, and thousands of Zags have found their home here because of your care and concern for them. All of us, and every one of your past advisees and students, are better off for having been accompanied in this life by you.  

Thank you for everything, and best wishes in all the days ahead!!! I wish you joy, peace, and only good surprises!

Love,
Peggy Sue

jane.story: I remember a Thanksgiving pot luck at your house on 28th street that took place not long after I arrived at GU.  It was such an amazing experience to join the SBA family and learn how much we all took care of each other.  The stories that were shared by colleagues sitting in front of your fireplace are a most awesome memory.
jane.message: Jane - I don't know what we are going to do without you.  The decades of information and experience that are leaving this building when you retire are irreplaceable.  I wish you best of luck in your new endeavors and hope that you will still come back to visit us from time to time.

jane.story: Jane, I wish I could share your closet as your outfits are always so stylish and perfectly put together. I hope that as I grow older, I can be like you, not just on the outside, but also in keeping such a kind and caring heart for everyone.
jane.message: Jane, wish you the best in the next chapter of your life, traveling, visiting grand kids, watching games, taking care of mom, etc. and having lots of fun! I will miss you so much!!!

Thank you so much for your amazing institutional knowledge and so much advising our students!  You will be missed!  I have attended many advising sessions from you where you tried to teach faculty the ins and outs of our curriculum nuances.  I have appreciated your warm smile.
Congratulations on your retirement! Wishing you endless weekends, slow mornings, and plenty of time for all the things you actually want to do! You will be sorely missed!!
Jane, you are the one who genuinely cares
Dear Jane, all our great results and achievements were thanks to your outstanding mentorship of our students (and, of course, of us as well). You will be deeply missed!

Jane, In many ways, you have been the heart of the School. You've poured yourself into this place and into the students who pass through it in a way that embodies what Gonzaga's Mission means. I genuinely cannot picture this school without you, but I'm comforted knowing you have shown us how to care for our students and one another. Wishing you every good thing.

jane.story: When I first started advising students, I had many questions. You were like an encyclopedia—always patient and knowledgeable—and your guidance gave me great confidence. I truly appreciate everything you’ve done.
jane.message: Congratulations on your retirement! Your willingness to help others has meant so much to me, especially when I was starting out. Wishing you a joyful and fulfilling next chapter.

I remember meeting you for dinner during my campus visit. You were extremely nice, friendly, and down to earth. I have enjoyed working with you over the years, and every smile you gave over the years has not gone unnoticed. You were always willing to help me with any advising issues. I will miss you! Wishing you joy and all good things as you go on from here. May you find peace with God and joy in all you do!

Congratulations on your retirement, Jane! I’m a little nervous about your departure because you’re so incredibly helpful when it comes to GU knowledge and helping students with their plans! You always answered my MANY questions super quickly! Thank you for always being so kind, helpful, and supportive. You have been a wonderful colleague to work with. It was always a pleasure to talk to you whenever we got a chance. I hope you have the best time in your new chapter! 🎉 -Mariella

jane.story: I will always have the memory of how you were able to get into the WSU game as a kid... pretty amazing story.
jane.message: What an amazing career of contributions to our students, faculty and university. You have played such a rich part of so many students' experiences and success while at GU and have done so with infectious energy and joy. Best of luck with the next steps on your journey, and I hope to continue seeing you around.

gary.message: Wishing you a happy and healthy retirement. Best regards. Ashish
I would have stuck with accounting if you had been my professor. I never thought I would be teaching accounting. Thank you for your support.

Thanks for being a part of my first few years at Gonzaga, Gary! I appreciated your calming voice in challenging discussions, your capacity to explain a Gonzaga approach to different questions, and your kindness in recognizing small accomplishments. We will miss you and wishing you the best!
Happy retirement! Wishing you health, happiness, and endless good times ahead.

Dear Gary.
You have been a very special part of the gift my work in the business school has been and the joy it has brought me.  I am humbled with gratitude.  I will always be grateful.   
Gary, I am honored to call you colleague and friend and I want you to know you are deeply valued and cherished, by me.  
Go play now!
With appreciation and affection,
Maureen

Congrats on retirement, Gary!
I admire how you made connections with students and faculty. I really enjoyed our conversation about Harry Potter and the studio tour you took in London. That quick chat before an SBA meeting meant a lot to me. I hope you have lots of fun adventures in retirement!
-Stephanie

Gary, thanks for being an awesome mentor, supporter, and for all your work over the years building our accounting program. I wouldn’t be where I am today without you and I’m so appreciative to have had you as a teacher for part of my journey through Gonzaga (as a student) and alongside me for these last (nearly) fifteen years!

gary.story: Gary can do all the things everywhere and all at once. Amazing ability to focus and reflect at the same time.
gary.message: You made this school phenomenal. We will continue your legacy of excellence.

gary.story: When I first interviewed at Gonzaga in 2001, Gary took me to dinner.  He called an audible and changed the location to a sports bar (instead of Clinkerdagger), so we could watch a GU basketball game while we chatted.  At first, I was bummed to miss out on a great meal (struggling PhD student); however, the food was still good, the company better, and I gained insight into Gary's passion for all things Gonzaga...even in those early years when the team was still considered a Cinderella.
gary.message: I have already expressed this to you a few years ago, but I really can't thank you enough for what you have done and meant to the accounting program here.  You've worked tirelessly in behalf of our students, faculty, accreditation, and all of our stakeholders.  Quite frankly, the accounting program would not be what it is today without you.  Thank you also for your friendship and support over these many years.  Wishing you a well-deserved and joyful retirement.

Gary, Your mentorship throughout my time at Gonzaga shaped me into the teacher I am today, and I will always be grateful for that. I meant what I said (during the HLA) that my goal is to be "just like Gary" when it comes to caring for others. I wish you much happiness in retirement!
Congratulations on your retirement, Gary! Congrats and cheers to your next adventure!
Gary, best of luck in retirement. I really enjoyed having you as a colleague and learning from you. Hopefully we'll still see you around campus or at some future GU games.

gary.story: Gary and I are both talkers, so all of my favorite memories involve conversations in our offices, Jepson hallways, Jack & Dan's, Rincon Tapatio, or the Osprey patio! I always appreciate his perspective, wisdom, and sense of humor, so he has been a go-to person for me when I need any of those!
gary.message:
G,

I am SO happy for you (and Jolanta!) that you have decided to move on to your future of great adventures! I have been missing your hearty laugh and big smile, your care and concern for me and my family, your unwavering focus on the students and elevating their success, your conscientiousness in everything you do, and your dedication to this place. You have been such a role model for me in how to be a great Gonzaga faculty member, and I hope that I can carry forward your legacy (albeit in a more fun discipline than yours)! Come back and teach marketing anytime!

Thank you for everything!!! I wish you joy, peace, and only good surprises in all the days ahead!

Love, 
Peggy Sue

PS See you in Vegas!

gary.story: I remember Gary's leadership of all our accounting department meetings after Kay retired.  He was tirelessly at work all the time from the wee hours of the morning until late at night.  Always looking out for the best for our department.
gary.message:
Gary, we miss you so much already.  I still remember my job interview at GU when you took me over to the CDA Resort for lunch.  It feels like it was yesterday.  Can't believe decades have gone by since then.  I hope you are enjoying the warmer weather (and yes, will be thinking about you every single time it snows here!).  Wishing you lots of fun and happiness in retirement.
-Sara

gary.story: Gary, where has the time gone? I still remember you as the welcoming person who greeted me at the Provost’s Office 22 years ago. I never imagined the day you would retire. It’s also special to me that we were both from Arizona.
gary.message: Wish you all the best in the next chapter of your life and keep having fun!
Thank you for your mentoring, your support, your advice, and your amazing work ethic. You are a wonderful colleague, and you will be missed!!

Congratulations on your retirement, Gary! You will be sorely missed by your colleagues and students. Thank you for your many years of dedication to accounting education and for the positive impact you have had on so many people. Wishing you a joyful and well-deserved retirement!

Gary,
Working with you in the Graduate School set the standard for me of what it looks like to be a professor in the SBA. The care you brought to students was impossible to miss, and it was no surprise to hear alumni say over and over how much your mentorship meant to them. You showed up for them, believed in them, and made a real difference in their lives.

You brought that same generosity to your colleagues. Your door was always open, and it was always clear how much you cared for us. It has been a privilege to work with you. You inspire me to show up more fully for my own students and colleagues. Wishing you all the best!
Congratulations on your retirement! Thank you for your contributions to the SBA.
I remember asking your class visit a while ago, and the gracious feedback and encouragement you gave me. Wishing you all the joy and peace that God gives. I’ll miss you!
Congratulations on your retirement, Gary! I hope this new chapter is filled with joy and happiness. It was a pleasure being your colleague. I hope you have the best time! 🎊 - Mariella

gary.story: I can still remember the first time I met you. You were so genuine in wanting to learn about who I was and gave me such impactful compliments that fueled my drive to grow. I have had the privilege to witness you doing the same for so many others. It's made a genuine difference in my journey, and I know the same goes for others.
gary.message: I hope the head-start on your retirement has gone well and you have been greatly missed here on campus.  Best of luck with enjoying your next chapter with Jolanta.

maureen.story: Maureen had big shoes to fill when Terry retired.  Instead of trying to be like her, I really appreciated that Maureen made the job hers by bringing her unique gifts and experiences to the work.

Maureen was a candidate for the position in the SBA for Assistant to the Dean. After 4 - 5 interviews Terry Coombes and I did a post review of the candidates. Terry was adamant and so right when she told me that if you hire Maureen she will bring positive leadership to her employment. Terry, you were right.
Maureen was so understanding about my late receipts... If I were Maureen, I would have tazed me!
Maureen and AJ were the dream team - I always picture the two of you going out for walks together at lunch time or the early afternoon.

Maureen, during our trip to London, we visited an art museum together, and I came to see the artist within you. I hope that part of you continues to grow and one day fully blossoms in your retirement.

My daughter Beth went through a phase where she really loved plants, especially succulents. Maureen always has a jungle going in her office (and in the lounge!) so I mentioned that to her, and she brought in a bunch of sweet little green things for Beth to nurture. Beth is off to college now, but I'm still taking care of the survivors! I think of Maureen every time I do.

maureen.message: Thank you Maureen for your support and caring "how are you doing" check-ins.  I'll miss our spontaneous chats in the faculty lounge on all subjects both personal and professional.  Enjoy your retirement!  I'm sure you will fill your days with fun projects and opportunities to be with friends and family.  We will miss you!
Maureen, you are full of life. :)

maureen.story: Maureen taught me so much when I took over Beta Alpha Psi and how to handle concur and expense reports. She's always so gracious and kind. I appreciate her patience.
maureen.message: Maureen- you've been so kind and patient with me over the years as I've learned concur and became the BAP advisor. Thank you for all you've done for me personally. You will be missed!
Thanks for helping me navigate the whole process. Even when we couldn't figure out what exactly HR wanted from us. Enjoy your next adventures!
Thank you for always having my back. I appreciate all the support. May your retirement be everything you've dreamed of and more! We will miss you <3

Maureen, You've been such a steady, welcoming presence in the SBA, which means more than I can easily put into words. Getting to work alongside you over the years and seeing you show up with such openness and genuine heart during our Truly Human Leadership cohort has been a gift. It's hard to imagine the SBA without you. Best wishes for your next adventure!

maureen.story: Maureen helped me propagate a piece of her Christmas cactus. It is now thriving and big, and flowers every winter :) I will always have a gift from her growing in my house.

maureen.message: Maureen thank you for all you've done for me and being the most encouraging and supportive boss I've ever had. We will miss your presence in the office so much. I hope you enjoy retirement and are able to make time for all your creative endeavors.
Congratulations on your retirement! Thank you so much for your support when I was starting out. Wishing you a wonderful and fulfilling next chapter.
You have been such a wonderful and helpful colleague. You always have a great smile on your face. I’ll miss seeing you around!

Congratulations on your retirement, Maureen! 🎊 Thank you for being such a helpful and supportive colleague. I’m grateful for all the guidance you gave me to navigate different processes. I’m not sure what we’ll do without you! 😅 But I hope you have a great time in your new chapter. Happy retirement! 🥳 -Mariella

Thank you so much for being our rock in the SBA and keeping things organized and moving forward.  You will be greatly missed but also, we are very happy for you and the next chapter of your journey.  Do not be a stranger.
